contaminant.name: Chromium Oxide Pitting
contaminant.description: Chrome-pitting forms when localized chromium oxide layer breaks down on plated surfaces because of environmental exposure and corrosion, so surface degradation happens in small pits. This contamination shows unique pitting patterns distinct from uniform rust or scaling, where isolated spots appear without spreading widely, and it behaves differently on chrome-plated steel by deepening quickly during cleaning, while on stainless steel, pits resist less but still challenge uniformity. Key removal issues arise from substrate damage risk, yet laser cleaning works effectively because precise pulses target oxide breakdown so contaminants ablate cleanly without affecting base material.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Chromium(III) Oxide and Chromium(VI) Oxide generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Chromium(VI) Oxide - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
